meta.description: Head-to-head eval set: agent vs naked-LLM control arm.

A_stateless.reference_explanation: The 把 construction moves the object before the verb to emphasise what is DONE TO that object: Subject + 把 + Object + Verb + (result/complement). The verb must be transitive and must carry something extra after it (a complement, 了, a result), because 把 stresses the disposal or effect on the object.
被 marks the passive: Receiver + 被 + (Agent) + Verb + (extra). The subject is the thing acted UPON. The agent after 被 is optional. Like 把, the verb usually needs a complement or 了 after it.
Verb + 了 marks that an action is completed or actualised, especially with a quantified or specific object: 我买了三本书. It is about completion of a specific event, not past tense in general.

A_stateless.input: 我今年二十岁。（when reporting a birthday just passed）
A_stateless.reference_explanation: Sentence-final 了 signals a new situation or change of state: 我饿了 (I'm hungry now), 天黑了 (it's gotten dark). It reports that something is now different from before.

A_stateless.input: 我吃了日本菜，很喜欢。（meaning: I have tried Japanese food）
A_stateless.reference_explanation: Verb + 过 says an experience has happened at least once in the past: 我去过中国 (I have been to China). It emphasises past experience, not a specific completed event.

A_stateless.input: 你怎么来了这里？（asking by what means）
A_stateless.reference_explanation: For a completed action, 是……的 emphasises a circumstance — when, where, how, or with whom: 我是昨天来的 (it was yesterday that I came). 是 goes before the emphasised element; 的 comes at the end.

Chinese requires a measure word between a number/demonstrative and a noun: Number + Measure Word + Noun. Different nouns take different measure words (本 for books, 个 for general items, 张 for flat things, 杯 for cups).
在 or 正在 before the verb marks an action in progress: 我在看书 (I am reading). 呢 can be added at the end for emphasis. This describes ongoing actions, not states.
Verb + 得 + complement describes how well or to what degree an action is done: 他跑得很快 (he runs fast). If the verb has an object, repeat the verb: 他说汉语说得很好.
All read 'de' but differ: 的 links a modifier to a noun (我的书), 地 links an adverbial to a verb (慢慢地走), 得 links a verb to a complement of degree/result (走得慢).
A + 比 + B + Adjective states A is more [adj] than B: 他比我高. Do NOT use 很/非常 with the adjective; use 更 or a concrete amount (高一点儿, 高得多).

A_stateless.rule_name: Word order: time and place before the verb
A_stateless.reference_explanation: Chinese puts time-when and place adverbials BEFORE the verb, not after: Subject + Time + Place + Verb + Object. 我明天在家看书, not 我看书在家明天.
Duration goes AFTER the verb: Verb + Duration. With an object, repeat the verb or place duration between verb and object: 我学汉语学了两年 / 我学了两年汉语.
Use 两 before a measure word to mean 'two of something' (两个人); use 二 for the number itself, counting, and ordinals (第二, 二十).

A_stateless.rule_name: 有点儿 (yǒudiǎnr) vs 一点儿 (yìdiǎnr)
A_stateless.reference_explanation: 有点儿 + Adjective goes before the adjective and implies something undesirable (有点儿贵 = a bit too expensive). Adjective + 一点儿 goes after and means 'a little more' as a comparison (便宜一点儿 = a little cheaper).

A_stateless.rule_name: 会 / 能 / 可以 (huì/néng/kěyǐ) modals
A_stateless.reference_explanation: 会 = a learned ability (会说汉语); 能 = physical capability or possibility in the moment (今天不能来); 可以 = permission (这里可以抽烟吗). They overlap but the core sense differs.
就 suggests something happens sooner/easily than expected (他七点就来了 = as early as 7); 才 suggests later/harder than expected (他九点才来 = not until 9). Note 就 often takes 了; 才 usually does not.
A second verb/adjective attaches to the main verb to show the RESULT: 看完 (finish reading), 听懂 (understand by listening), 写错 (write wrongly). Negate with 没 (没看完), not 不.
来/去 and directional verbs (上下进出回过) attach to a verb to show direction relative to the speaker: 进来 (come in), 拿出去 (take out). A place object goes before 来/去: 回家来.

A_stateless.input: 因为下雨，我没去。（acceptable, but learners often force English single-conjunction）
A_stateless.rule_name: 因为……所以…… (yīnwèi...suǒyǐ) cause and result
A_stateless.reference_explanation: Paired conjunctions are common in Chinese: 因为 (because) in the first clause, 所以 (so) in the second. Using both is natural, unlike English which uses only one.

A_stateless.expected_correction: 虽然很累，但是我很开心。
A_stateless.reference_explanation: 虽然 (although) pairs with 但是/可是 (but) in the following clause. Both are used together in Chinese.
还是 is 'or' in questions offering a choice (你喝茶还是咖啡？); 或者 is 'or' in statements (我喝茶或者咖啡都行).

A_stateless.input: 我在这儿住了三年。（meaning: and still live here）
A_stateless.rule_name: Verb + 了 + object + 了 (ongoing up to now)
A_stateless.reference_explanation: Two 了 with a duration mean an action has been going on and continues: 我学了两年汉语了 (I have been studying Chinese for two years and still am). The sentence-final 了 adds the 'up to now, still true' sense.
呢 turns a noun/pronoun into a 'what about...?' follow-up question when a topic is already established: 我很好，你呢？(I'm fine, and you?).

A_stateless.expected_correction_alternatives: 书被他借走了。 / 她把书借给了他。
A_stateless.rule_name: Mixing 把 and 被 in one clause
A_stateless.reference_explanation: 把 = subject acts on object; 被 = subject is acted upon. Choose one based on which noun is the topic.

A_stateless.input: 小时候我每天喝了牛奶。
A_stateless.rule_name: Treating 了 as a general past-tense marker
A_stateless.reference_explanation: 了 marks completion of a specific event, not past tense. Habitual past takes no 了.
Number/demonstrative + Measure Word + Noun is obligatory in Chinese.

A_stateless.rule_name: Overusing 个 as a default measure word
A_stateless.reference_explanation: 个 is general but many nouns need a specific measure word (本 books, 张 flat items, 只 animals).

A_stateless.expected_correction_alternatives: 他比我高。 / 他比我高得多。
A_stateless.rule_name: Using 很/非常 in a 比 comparison
A_stateless.reference_explanation: For degree in a 比 sentence use 更, or a quantity like 一点儿 / 得多.

A_stateless.rule_name: English word order for time and place
A_stateless.reference_explanation: Chinese: Subject + Time + Place + Verb + Object.
的 (noun modifier), 地 (adverb + verb), 得 (verb + degree complement).

A_stateless.expected_correction: 虽然很贵，但是我还是买了。
A_stateless.expected_correction_alternatives: 虽然很贵，但是我还是买了。
A_stateless.rule_name: Dropping the second half of a paired conjunction
A_stateless.reference_explanation: Chinese commonly keeps both: 虽然……但是, 因为……所以, 不但……而且.

A_stateless.input: 我去了法国，很漂亮。（meaning: I have been to France）
A_stateless.rule_name: Using 了 where experiential 过 is meant
A_stateless.reference_explanation: 过 = have ever experienced; 了 = a specific completed event.

A_stateless.rule_name: 把 sentence with a bare verb
A_stateless.reference_explanation: 把 + Object + Verb + (complement/了/result). A bare verb is ungrammatical.

A_stateless.rule_name: Using 能 for a learned skill
A_stateless.reference_explanation: 会 = acquired skill; 能 = capability/possibility right now; 可以 = permission.
Duration (how long) goes AFTER the verb; repeat the verb if there is an object.

A_stateless.rule_name: Using 或者 in a choice question
A_stateless.reference_explanation: 还是 = 'or' in choice questions; 或者 = 'or' in statements.

A_stateless.rule_name: Negating a completed/resultative verb with 不
A_stateless.reference_explanation: 没 negates completion/result and past events; 不 negates habits, willingness, and present/future.

A_stateless.rule_name: Inserting 是 before a predicate adjective
A_stateless.reference_explanation: Adjectives are predicates on their own; use 很 (often bleached) not 是.

A_stateless.rule_name: Using 吗 for a 'what about you' follow-up
A_stateless.reference_explanation: 呢 asks 'what about X?' when the topic is already set; 吗 makes a yes/no question.
